content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      : File                                                  Date  : November 23, 1977

From  : Barbara Campbell /K
       FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

Subject: Advice on Government Code Section 84202

       Jim McKelvey, the Fresno City Attorney, called on
November 17, 1977, to ask what "deadline" meant in
Government Code Section 84202, in connection with a local
recall drive.

       The circulator of a Fresno recall has until November 29,
1977, to submit the petitions. Under Election Code Section
87212 the clerk must ascertain within 20 days after the recall
petition is filed whether there is the requisite number of
signatures. If the petition is certified as insufficient,
Election Code Section 87214 provides there may be ten additional
days, after the date of the insufficiency certification, within
which to supplement the petition.

       Mr. McKelvey wondered whether the deadline would be
November 29, 1977, or the date ten days after the Clerk makes
the certification of insufficiency. Mr. McKelvey is quite
certain that such a certification will be made in the case of
this particular recall attempt. After consultation with
Ted Prim and Mike Baker on this issue, I advised Mr. McKelvey
on November 18, 1977, that the deadline for campaign state-
ments for committees supporting or opposing the qualification
of that recall would be 35 days after the petitions were
finally submitted, which would not occur until after the ten
supplemental days allowed by Election Code Section 87214 had
run.

sections.question: What 'deadline' meant in Government Code Section 84202 in connection with a local recall drive, and specifically whether the deadline would be November 29, 1977, or the date ten days after the Clerk makes the certification of insufficiency.
sections.conclusion: The deadline for campaign statements for committees supporting or opposing the qualification of that recall would be 35 days after the petitions were finally submitted, which would not occur until after the ten supplemental days allowed by Election Code Section 87214 had run.
sections.question_synthetic: The Fresno City Attorney asked whether the campaign statement deadline under Government Code Section 84202 for a recall petition would be November 29, 1977, or ten days after the clerk certifies the petition as insufficient, given the extension provisions in Election Code Section 87214.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the campaign statement deadline is 35 days after petitions are finally submitted, meaning it runs from the date after the ten-day supplemental period under Election Code Section 87214 expires, not from the initial November 29, 1977 deadline.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Memorandum format without traditional section headers; question and conclusion content extracted from narrative text. Document is undated in header but dated November 23, 1977. Advice was given verbally on November 18, 1977 per the memo.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What 'deadline' meant in Government Code Section 84202 in connection with a local recall drive, and specifically whether the deadline would be November 29, 1977, or the date ten days after the Clerk makes the certification of insufficiency.

CONCLUSION: The deadline for campaign statements for committees supporting or opposing the qualification of that recall would be 35 days after the petitions were finally submitted, which would not occur until after the ten supplemental days allowed by Election Code Section 87214 had run.

The circulator of a Fresno recall has until November 29, 1977, to submit the petitions. Under Election Code Section 87212 the clerk must ascertain within 20 days after the recall petition is filed whether there is the requisite number of signatures. If the petition is certified as insufficient, Election Code Section 87214 provides there may be ten additional days, after the date of the insufficiency certification, within which to supplement the petition. Mr. McKelvey wondered whether the deadline would be November 29, 1977, or the date ten days after the Clerk makes the certification of insufficiency. Mr. McKelvey is quite certain that such a certification will be made in the case of this particular recall attempt. After consultation with Ted Prim and Mike Baker on this issue, I advised Mr. McKelvey on November 18, 1977, that the deadline for campaign state- ments for committees supporting or opposing the qualification of that recall would be 35 days after the petitions were finally submitted, which would not occur until after the ten supplemental days allowed by Election Code Section 87214 had run.
embedding.summary: FPPC provided advice to the Fresno City Attorney regarding the campaign statement deadline for a local recall drive, clarifying that the 35-day deadline runs from when petitions are finally submitted, including any supplemental filing periods allowed by Election Code Section 87214.
